author: PC Gamer
text:
There are already two mods to make Gothic Remake's lockpicking less painful
Sorry, Fingers, you've been superseded.

author: PC Gamer
text:
I have played what I suspect will be a perfect videogame
Hands-on with the next N+.

author: PC Gamer
text:
There's suddenly a terrifying number of games releasing this fall
I'm going to need to fit a few extra weeks into September somehow.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The new Crazy Taxi has a generative AI disclosure on its Steam page, and people are not happy
"More like Lazy Taxi."

author: PC Gamer
text:
Every game, trailer, and announcement in the PC Gaming Show 2026
Every Space Marine, vampire, and weird mushroom guy accounted for.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Into the Wind is a gorgeous, Ghibli-esque adventure about taking over your missing uncle's delivery service, but with more dogfighting in your aeroplane bike
It's a bike, it's a plane, it's delivery-man!

author: PC Gamer
text:
Oh, yep, that's a pregnant nun chasing you in the gameplay trailer for Remothered: Red Nun's Legacy
I'd run away too.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Robots and matter-altering powers help you get the edge in combat and exploration, in a new Exodus extended gameplay reveal
Here are the highlights from the time-dilating sci-fi action RPG.

author: PC Gamer
text:
If you've ever wanted to play Geoguessr with a narrative twist, Locator: The Search for Abigail is what you've been waiting for
Put those detective skills to the test.

author: PC Gamer
text:
If you've ever thought to yourself 'Yeah, me and my mates could probably take on Batman' then P.O.N is the next horror game you should check out
Never meet your heroes.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Everything I saw on my trip to Valheim's final biome, the Deep North: from ghost vikings to rampaging moose
Met a seal, refused to kill it, then died to a moose. Nature is beautiful.

author: PC Gamer
text:
After half a decade, Valheim 1.0 finally has a release date
From reveal to release, it's been a hell of a journey.

author: PC Gamer
text:
We're getting a new Vampire: The Masquerade game and, hell yes, it's a 'narrative-driven CRPG' with big Disco Elysium vibes
These are all my keywords.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Gears of War: E-Day gets the official Cliff Bleszinski seal of approval
He sounded doubtful at first, but once the E-Day Direct got rolling, the Gears of War creator changed his tune.

author: PC Gamer
text:
About Fishing keeps reeling me in further with its creepy, nostalgic vibes
I have a sneaking suspicion this game isn't just about fishing.

author: PC Gamer
text:
We got a fresh look at Total War: Warhammer 40k and, friends, I'm already on board to do some horrible things to millions of spacemen
Like all good games, Total War: Warhammer 40k has me eager to do war crimes.

author: PC Gamer
text:
From the creators of Mouthwashing comes a co-op tank game with a disgusting twist
Be prepared for some very weird sights.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Dave the Diver becomes a real boy in this new live-action trailer for the upcoming In the Jungle DLC
And look at the size of those animals!

author: PC Gamer
text:
Armatus, the demonic roguelite where you start strong and just get stronger, is coming this winter
I wonder if that warrior knows what a smartphone is?

author: PC Gamer
text:
This co-op clown cacophony crams over 80 minigames into a single videogame
That's game night sorted for the next few weeks at least.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Indie climbing game rebrands and debuts unconventional tools like pogo sticks to help you reach the Sunset Summit
I hope you aren't afraid of heights.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Pipes.exe is a co-op horror game about the world's favourite screensaver
No, not the flying toasters.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Action roguelite To Kill a God gets a new mage character, and a demo for you to try them out in
Put down the sword and bow, pick up a staff.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Co-op RPG Terrinoth: Heroes of Descent brings the board game to PC—and it's available to play right now
This is the game's first foray onto PC.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Beast of Reincarnation has some stellar voice actors, but one has to play protagonist Emma completely devoid of emotion
It's harder than you think.

author: PC Gamer
text:
A shooter we called 'one of the boldest, freshest games' of 2023 is getting a sequel: El Paso, Elsewhere 2 sends a slo-mo vampire into the void
The stakes are high.

author: PC Gamer
text:
We've just got a new look at 'the sequel to the award winning strategy game', Duskers 2.0, which promises higher stakes and a more emotive story
Explore. Adapt. Revive.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Off-road racing? Psh, try off-world racing: Exo Rally Championship just launched on Steam
The extraterrestrial racing game has arrived with customizable rovers and a galaxy-spanning career mode.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Oh hell yes, Nightdive's next delicious remaster treat is Thief
4K moss arrows just like I always dreamed.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Outward 2, the sequel to a survival action RPG we gave an 89% to, is in open beta now
Alright, I'm heading out(ward).

author: PC Gamer
text:
There are already two mods to make Gothic Remake's lockpicking less painful
Sorry, Fingers, you've been superseded.

author: PC Gamer
text:
I have played what I suspect will be a perfect videogame
Hands-on with the next N+.

author: PC Gamer
text:

Alright, I'm heading out(ward).

author: PC Gamer
text:
Xbox lost 'millions' of Game Pass subscribers after last year's massive price hike
This would explain why Asha Sharma moved so quickly to lower them.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Famous game director Fumito Ueda says his studio does not use AI for 'game development'
The studio behind the upcoming Gen Atlas uses AI for project management tasks, but that's it, says the game's director.

author: PC Gamer
text:
15 minutes is all you need in this roguelike action RPG to feel like a maxed-out Diablo god laying waste to an entire screen of monsters
Clyde Games' dark fantasy action game Cursemark is for all the busy action RPG freaks out there.

author: PC Gamer
text:
A Star Trek horror game is just wild enough to work, but not the one Bloober Team is making
A Star Trek horror game is just wild enough to work, but not the one Bloober Team is making

author: PC Gamer
text:
5 cozy game demos I'm downloading right now after I counted a whole 37 dropping during Summer Game Fest weekend
Showcase weekend was stuffed to the stitches with cozy game demos so I've tallied them up and sorted them out.

author: PC Gamer
text:
One of the biggest VR developers in the business closes a studio: 'The VR market remains a challenging space'
Thief VR publisher Vertigo Games has been a VR-exclusive studio for years, but its most recent release, and its next game, are both non-VR titles.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Fumito Ueda on why Gen Atlas has shooting but isn't a shooter, cool robots, and generative AI
"It's not to score points, but it's there so that it matters, and you feel that you do exist in that world."

author: PC Gamer
text:
After seeing Thief Remastered, I'm calling it: That doomed Deux Ex remaster should get rebooted with Nightdive in charge
It just makes sense.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The best Amazon Prime Day 2026 gaming headset deals: I'm hunting down the best-sounding cans for the lowest prices
Treat yourself to a new pair of cans this Amazon Prime Day.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Studio says 'not every publisher' is swerving GTA 6's release and declares a November 5 release date for its game: 'Maybe we're just not afraid enough'
That's one way to gin up buzz.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Where to find Caine in Gothic Remake
Complete The Lost Apprentice and track down Caine.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Hellblade studio kills its mysterious 'horrors of the mind' project so it can make a new Senua game instead
Senua, announced during Summer Game Fest, is not Hellblade 3.

author: PC Gamer
text:
A developer got the original Half-Life running on a 2007 Nokia N95 at a respectable 30 fps, plus it even works with a mouse and keyboard
Maybe it's time for that replay?

author: PC Gamer
text:
It's fine if the visitors to your theme park die in this sim: it's actually the whole point
Everyone's gotta eat.

author: PC Gamer
text:
What's the plan with Halo, Xbox?
Despite Xbox living in its nostalgia era, its showcase failed to give Halo a promising future.

author: PC Gamer
text:
After everyone hated his AI Prada ad, Hideo Kojima says he's 'not interested' in AI
To be fair, I also rapidly lose interest in things once I've already been paid for them.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Summer Game Fest no-shows: The big games that were absent from 2026's showcases
Who's out for Summer? Who's out forever?

author: PC Gamer
text:
A new MMO announcement is more exciting than all the other SGF news combined
Thanks, Guild Wars 3.

author: PC Gamer
text:
After huge delays, will The Wolf Among Us 2 make you wait between episodes? CEO says 'We live in a Netflix era. People can't wait'
Wolfing it down.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Don't let Deadlock's MOBA tag scare you off—there's a quick play mode for dummies which is the perfect entry point
Don't let the idea of playing a MOBA frighten you away.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Fumito Ueda knows that you're worried about Gen Atlas's framerate
"We did our best we could in delivering this trailer for this moment."

author: PC Gamer
text:
Games Workshop showed us The Emperor's current state for the first time in 38 years and it's hard to overstate how monumental that is
If I have that many teeth left after 10,000 years, I'll chalk it up as a win.

author: PC Gamer
text:
How to open Scatty's chest in Gothic Remake
Sneak into Scatty's hut, lockpick his chest, and steal his ring.

author: PC Gamer
text:
There are already two mods to make Gothic Remake's lockpicking less painful
Sorry, Fingers, you've been superseded.

I have played what I suspect will be a perfect videogame
Hands-on with the next N+.

There's suddenly a terrifying number of games releasing this fall
I'm going to need to fit a few extra weeks into September somehow.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Generative AI 'was only used as a reference,' Crazy Taxi creator says: Artists would 'look at that generated image and draw the actual thing'
I don't think this really improves the situation.
